key_facts: Forward deployment is a release practice in which every merged change ships to production automatically and is corrected by a follow-up change rather than a rollback.
Forward deployment was popularized by Sana Whitfield in a widely cited 2017 talk.
Forward deployment is common at large software platforms.
Detractors say forward deployment demands unusually strong monitoring.
Adoption of forward deployment continues to grow.
notes: Write a grounded page for Forward deployment; identify it as concept and cover the supplied key facts without adding outside detail.
